subject: Pin jnr-unixsocket for Docker plugin to 0.13
commit_message:
Pin jnr-unixsocket for Docker plugin to 0.13

The current verison of jnr-unixsocket is 0.12 from the Docker
plugin however in order to be able the images for ARM64, it needs
the newer version which has support for it.

There is work upstream to switch the version to 0.18[0] however
it seems that this is still pending, so we can pin it for now until
that merges.

[0]: https://github.com/spotify/docker-maven-plugin/pull/379

Change-Id: I447a70622049d70e365cf5c37d3831abd8d86889
Issue-ID: CIMAN-154
Signed-off-by: Mohammed Naser <mnaser@vexxhost.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-platform-inventory-api-master-verify-java/94/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/dcaegen2-platform-inventory-api-master-verify-java/94

Patch Set 1:

Thanks Mohammed for this work.  I have couple comments/questions:

1) Want to double confirm that jnr-unixsocket:0.13 provides ARM64 support?  Because the github issue you linked seems to indicate that you would need to use 0.18 so why only pin on 0.13?
2) This is more for me.  When I do a "mvn dependency:tree" on the docker-maven-plugin, I see "[INFO] |  +- com.github.jnr:jnr-unixsocket:jar:0.8:compile".  How are you seeing 0.12 being used?

These questions also apply to the service change handler project where the changes are the same.

Patch Set 1:

Hey Michael

1) It does, the exact patch in question is the following:
https://github.com/jnr/jnr-unixsocket/commit/767ed30357a1c21ecfd6b474142e01c10a5c5c8b

This bumps the usage of jnr-ffi to 2.1.0 which includes the following patch

https://github.com/jnr/jnr-ffi/commit/4bf608dcdf4820a05dc9338bbd1a33dd9550ae2b

The dependency tree doesn't show it because it actually is a dependency that's put on the plugin itself, if it was a project dependency, it would be great and easy to add to oparent, but hence, it's not :(

Patch Set 1:

> 1) It does, the exact patch in question is the following:
 > https://github.com/jnr/jnr-unixsocket/commit/767ed30357a1c21ecfd6b474142e01c10a5c5c8b
 > 
 > This bumps the usage of jnr-ffi to 2.1.0 which includes the
 > following patch
 > 
 > https://github.com/jnr/jnr-ffi/commit/4bf608dcdf4820a05dc9338bbd1a33dd9550ae2b

OK.  Thanks for the links and great investigation

 > The dependency tree doesn't show it because it actually is a
 > dependency that's put on the plugin itself...
